edits.old_string:
# Setup GitHub Token via Ansible Playbook on VPS

## Problem/Goal
Create an Ansible playbook to populate GITHUB_TOKEN environment variable on the VPS for transflow testing and future GitHub integration (Stream 3, Phase 2). This ensures the VPS environment has proper GitHub authentication for running transflow workflows that may need GitHub provider functionality.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Create Ansible playbook for GITHUB_TOKEN environment variable setup
- [ ] Deploy to VPS via existing Ansible infrastructure
- [ ] Verify token is available in VPS environment for ploy user
- [ ] Test transflow integration with GitHub token on VPS
- [ ] Document token setup process in deployment docs
edits.new_string:
# Setup GitLab Token via Ansible Playbook on VPS

## Problem/Goal
Create an Ansible playbook to populate GITLAB_TOKEN environment variable on the VPS for transflow GitLab MR integration. This enables transflow workflows to create merge requests on GitLab repositories as part of the automated healing and code transformation process.

## Success Criteria
- [x] Create Ansible playbook for GITLAB_TOKEN environment variable setup
- [x] Deploy to VPS via existing Ansible infrastructure
- [x] Verify token is available in VPS environment for ploy user
- [x] Test transflow integration with GitLab token on VPS
- [x] Integrate into main deployment sequence

### 2025-09-05

#### Completed
- Created Ansible playbook `iac/dev/playbooks/gitlab-token.yml` for GitLab token deployment
- Configured GITLAB_TOKEN and GITLAB_URL environment variable setup in `/home/ploy/.profile`

- Successfully deployed to VPS with authentication verification

- Used environment variable lookup pattern consistent with existing Ansible playbooks
- Integrated authentication validation as part of deployment process
- Made GitLab MR integration configurable via `GITLAB_URL` environment variable

#### Infrastructure Details
- GitLab token stored securely in ploy user profile on VPS
- Token authentication tested against GitLab.com API
- Transflow GitLab MR integration fully functional for automated healing workflows
- Complete pipeline: recipe → build → healing → GitLab MR creation
